edible_parts: Young needles/tips (vitamin-C tea); inner bark (famine food).
other_uses: Graceful shade-tolerant conifer of cool ravines; the state tree of Pennsylvania.
prep: Steep the young needles and tips for a fragrant vitamin-C tea; the inner bark was a survival food and tannin source. A majestic shade tree (note: threatened by woolly adelgid).
soil: Moist, cool, acidic, well-drained soils; shade tolerant.
risks: Not to be confused with poison-hemlock the herb — this conifer is safe as tea.
buy: Native-plant nurseries and native seed suppliers.
